sourceNotes: Historical-inspired, fictionalized for gameplay; does not use real party names in playable text.
New batch covers 2001-2004 with expanded categories: corruption/scams, sports, economy (positive), welfare, health, environment, and culture.

Anchors include chit fund collapses, PDS scams, teacher recruitment fraud, Kolkata Derby, Santosh Trophy, IT park growth, jute industry revival, Darjeeling tea exports, polio eradication, tribal literacy, and biometric ration pilot.
Each item carries unique, context-calibrated reaction options, weights, risk chances, and effects reflecting the period's political and social dynamics.

defaults.reactionRule: Each news item contains its own 3 active reactions plus no_comment. Reaction weights and effects vary by issue profile.
defaults.thirdPartyReactionRule: THIRD_PARTY can use opposition-compatible and neutral reactions.

newsItems.title: Chit Fund Collapse Leaves Thousands Of Small Depositors Stranded
newsItems.description: A Kolkata-based chit fund operator vanishes after collecting crores from daily-wage workers and pensioners across several districts.

reactionOptions.text: Demand immediate police action and arrest of the accused promoters.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as opportunistic blaming without alternative plan.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a state relief fund to compensate victims immediately.

risk.badOutcome: Government says no funds available; proposal collapses.

reactionOptions.text: Call for legislation to regulate informal deposit schemes.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as slow and bureaucratic response to victims in crisis.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Kolkata Derby Draws Record Crowd; Fans Demand Better Stadium Safety
newsItems.description: The East Bengal vs Mohun Bagan match fills Salt Lake Stadium to capacity. Post-match crowd crush raises safety questions, but local pride runs high.

reactionOptions.text: Celebrate the match as proof of Bengal's sporting culture and promise stadium upgrades.

risk.badOutcome: Critics say stadium promises are hollow election rhetoric.

reactionOptions.text: Order a crowd safety audit of the stadium and publish findings.

risk.badOutcome: Audit report delayed; press calls it lip service.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the government allocate more funds for grassroots football and sports infrastructure.

risk.badOutcome: Government dismisses demand as non-priority.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Midday Meal Scheme Expansion Boosts School Enrolment In Backward Blocks
newsItems.description: District reports show a measurable jump in primary school attendance after the state extended cooked midday meals to more blocks, with mothers citing child nutrition as a key reason.

reactionOptions.text: Hold a school rally and publicly credit the scheme for bringing children back to class.

risk.badOutcome: Media finds nutrition quality complaints; credit turns to embarrassment.

reactionOptions.text: Push for expansion to upper primary and tribal schools.

risk.badOutcome: Budget constraints make promise undeliverable.

reactionOptions.text: Warn that contractor corruption may undermine scheme quality; demand independent monitoring.

risk.badOutcome: Government shows clean audit; opposition looks cynical.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Ration Dealer Network Exposed For Diverting PDS Grain To Black Market
newsItems.description: Investigative reports in Kolkata dailies detail how below-poverty-line grain was being sold illegally by a network of licensed dealers, with officials allegedly looking the other way.

reactionOptions.text: Announce suspension of accused dealers and a state-wide PDS audit.

risk.badOutcome: Audit stalls and accused dealers return; looks like cover-up.

reactionOptions.text: Call for a legislative committee inquiry into PDS mismanagement.

risk.badOutcome: Government blocks the inquiry; opposition looks powerless.

reactionOptions.text: Propose technology-based ration cards to eliminate ghost beneficiaries.

risk.badOutcome: Proposal dismissed as impractical for rural areas.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cyclone Relief Funds Allegedly Siphoned Off In Coastal Districts
newsItems.description: Affected families in Medinipur and South 24 Parganas report receiving no compensation months after the coastal storm, while local leaders are seen buying new goods.

reactionOptions.text: Announce direct government-to-family disbursement bypassing local middlemen.

risk.badOutcome: Implementation delayed; families wait weeks longer.

reactionOptions.text: Lead a protest march of victims to district headquarters.

risk.badOutcome: March turns violent; police use force; optics shift.

reactionOptions.text: Share victim testimonies with the press and demand a judicial probe.

risk.badOutcome: Government denies allegations and press backs off.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Bengal's Santosh Trophy Victory Sparks Statewide Celebration
newsItems.description: The state football team claims the national Santosh Trophy title after a decade-long drought, with spontaneous celebrations in Kolkata and district towns.

reactionOptions.text: Felicitate the players at Raj Bhavan and announce sports scholarships.

risk.badOutcome: Scholarship funds don't materialise; trust erodes.

reactionOptions.text: Use the win to push for a grassroots sports fund in every district.

risk.badOutcome: No budget allocated; seen as empty promise.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the government modernise district stadiums while the sporting mood is high.

risk.badOutcome: Government accuses opposition of hijacking the moment.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Kolkata Port Records Strong Cargo Growth, Raising Hopes For Trade Revival
newsItems.description: Port Authority reports double-digit cargo volume growth for the second consecutive quarter, with regional trade analysts flagging new opportunities in Bangladesh and Southeast Asia.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a state trade corridor strategy to maximise port growth.

risk.badOutcome: Industry groups call plan vague; implementation questions mount.

reactionOptions.text: Credit port workers and unions for the growth and call for wage revision.

risk.badOutcome: Wage demand triggers management pushback; growth risks stall.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that port revenue be linked to local employment and skills training.

risk.badOutcome: Government says demand is outside state jurisdiction.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Hospital Drug Procurement Scam Angers Doctors And Patients
newsItems.description: Sub-standard medicines allegedly purchased at inflated prices from politically-linked suppliers are found in government hospital stores in at least three districts.

reactionOptions.text: Suspend the procurement process and order independent drug quality testing.

risk.badOutcome: Medicine shortages in hospitals worsen; crisis deepens.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a public inquiry with doctors' representatives and patient groups.

risk.badOutcome: Inquiry denied; opposition looks like it's politicising health.

reactionOptions.text: Champion the doctor who leaked documents and promise whistleblower protection.

risk.badOutcome: Whistleblower recants under pressure; story unravels.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Jute Industry Gets National Package; Workers Cautiously Hopeful
newsItems.description: The central government announces a relief-and-modernisation package for jute mills amid mounting closures. Bengal mill workers are cautiously optimistic but unions demand job security guarantees.

reactionOptions.text: Negotiate to ensure Bengal gets maximum share of the central package.

risk.badOutcome: Package funds are delayed; mills close before money arrives.

reactionOptions.text: Hold a mill-town rally with workers to press for job security clauses.

risk.badOutcome: Management warns rally disrupts production; media sides with management.

reactionOptions.text: Release a state jute modernisation plan to attract private investment.

risk.badOutcome: Unions oppose privatisation angle; plan stalls.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: SC/ST Scholarship Funds Diverted By District Officials, Probe Ordered
newsItems.description: A state audit finds lakhs in scholarship money intended for SC/ST students unaccounted for in several district education offices, with ghost beneficiaries listed on rolls.

reactionOptions.text: Dismiss and prosecute the responsible district officials immediately.

risk.badOutcome: Officials are reinstated after union pressure; looks like backtracking.

reactionOptions.text: Hold open sessions with SC/ST communities to identify victims and ensure direct repayment.

risk.badOutcome: Sessions are poorly attended; communities remain distrustful.

reactionOptions.text: Use the audit findings to expose systemic corruption and demand a legislative debate.

risk.badOutcome: Government dismisses findings as exaggerated; opposition misquotes numbers.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Women's Self-Help Group Network Reaches Milestone Of One Lakh Members
newsItems.description: A state-backed microfinance initiative celebrating its one-lakh membership mark reports loan repayment rates above ninety percent; women leaders credit it for transforming rural household economics.

reactionOptions.text: Launch a statewide SHG expansion campaign and connect groups to government schemes.

risk.badOutcome: Rapid expansion dilutes quality; defaults begin to rise.

reactionOptions.text: Celebrate women SHG leaders publicly and propose them as panchayat candidates.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as tokenism if women candidates lack real decision-making power.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the SHG model be extended to urban poor and migrant women.

risk.badOutcome: Urban rollout fraught with administrative issues; promise backfires.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Industrial Land Acquisition Protest Turns Violent In Howrah District
newsItems.description: Farmers resist forced acquisition of multi-crop land for a proposed chemical plant; three protesters are injured in a police lathi-charge.

reactionOptions.text: Order suspension of acquisition and set up a farmer-government dialogue committee.

risk.badOutcome: Industry investors pull out; accused of killing development.

reactionOptions.text: Stand with farmers and demand proper compensation and consent.

risk.badOutcome: Industry lobby accuses opposition of blocking progress.

reactionOptions.text: Send a cross-party mediation team to diffuse tensions.

risk.badOutcome: Mediation collapses; both sides accuse the team of bias.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Housing Board Scam: Flats Allotted To Ghost Beneficiaries, Audit Shows
newsItems.description: A state housing board audit reveals hundreds of low-income flats were allotted to ineligible applicants, including relatives of government employees, while genuine applicants waited for years.

reactionOptions.text: Cancel fraudulent allotments and re-run a transparent lottery for genuine applicants.

risk.badOutcome: Cancelled allottees go to court; process stalls for years.

reactionOptions.text: Organise a rally of cheated applicants and hand a petition to the CM.

risk.badOutcome: Petition ignored; mobilisation fatigue sets in.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a shift to computerised, online allotment to prevent future fraud.

risk.badOutcome: Technical implementation fails; new system crashes on launch.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Eden Gardens Hosts India-England Test Amid Packed Stands And Political Pageantry
newsItems.description: The historic cricket ground hosts a packed Test match; politicians jostle for box seats while fans from districts travel overnight. The match becomes a moment of state pride despite ongoing controversies.

reactionOptions.text: Organise free public viewing events in districts for those who cannot travel.

risk.badOutcome: Power cuts during broadcast; public events become embarrassing.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the ground be upgraded to international standards to attract more fixtures.

risk.badOutcome: Upgrade funds not available; demand seen as hollow.

reactionOptions.text: Announce district cricket coaching scholarships linked to the buzz around the match.

risk.badOutcome: Scholarship programme lacks funding and fades after announcement.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Police Extortion Racket Targeting Market Vendors Exposed In Kolkata
newsItems.description: A sting operation by a Bengali newspaper documents policemen collecting weekly payments from Hawkers in multiple Kolkata markets, with money allegedly flowing up the chain of command.

reactionOptions.text: Transfer implicated officers immediately and set up an anti-corruption vigilance cell.

risk.badOutcome: Transfers seen as punishment postings; practice continues under new officers.

reactionOptions.text: Set up a vendor complaint hotline and publicise it widely.

risk.badOutcome: Hotline receives calls but no action follows; trust destroyed.

reactionOptions.text: Raise the expose in the assembly and demand a police accountability commission.

risk.badOutcome: Government tables the debate; motion fails; opposition looks weak.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Darjeeling Tea Fetches Record Auction Price; State Celebrates Export Milestone
newsItems.description: A first-flush Darjeeling tea lot sells at a record price per kilogram at Kolkata auction, reviving hopes for the state's famed tea industry and hillside workers.

reactionOptions.text: Launch a Darjeeling tea branding and GI protection campaign.

risk.badOutcome: Blending fraud by some estates undercuts GI credibility.

reactionOptions.text: Demand estate workers share in the price windfall through a wage revision.

risk.badOutcome: Estate owners threaten to cut production; situation escalates.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an integrated eco-tourism-tea package for the hills.

risk.badOutcome: Hill communities resist plan as outside imposition.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Illegal Coal Mining Royalty Fraud Exposed In Raniganj Belt
newsItems.description: Investigations reveal a network of mine operators understating production figures to evade royalty payments, with officials allegedly receiving a cut in Burdwan and Birbhum.

reactionOptions.text: Set up a special task force to audit all mining royalties in the belt.

risk.badOutcome: Task force is stacked with implicated officials; whitewash allegations fly.

reactionOptions.text: Demand CAG audit of the state mining department and publish findings.

risk.badOutcome: Audit delayed by bureaucratic obstruction; story fades.

reactionOptions.text: Tie the scam to worker welfare — lost royalties mean no funds for miner housing or safety.

risk.badOutcome: Mine owners dispute link; workers divided.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Early Warning System Helps Evacuate Coastal Families Before Cyclone Landfall
newsItems.description: District administration successfully evacuates over fifty thousand families from low-lying coastal areas before a cyclone makes landfall. Casualties are minimal and officials receive national praise.

reactionOptions.text: Publicly honour the district officials and showcase the model nationally.

risk.badOutcome: Controversy over who takes credit mars celebrations.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the early warning system be expanded to all coastal blocks.

risk.badOutcome: Funding gap means expansion is delayed.

reactionOptions.text: Acknowledge success while demanding faster post-cyclone rehabilitation.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as grudging credit-stealing; press criticises tone.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Hawker Eviction Drive Turns Violent In Kolkata; Opposition Calls It Political Targeting
newsItems.description: A municipality-led hawker eviction drive in central Kolkata turns ugly when street traders resist. Opposition claims hawkers with rival-party links are specifically targeted.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a proper hawker rehabilitation and designated-zone policy.

risk.badOutcome: Designated zones are too far from customers; hawkers reject plan.

reactionOptions.text: Stand with hawkers and challenge the eviction drive as anti-poor.

risk.badOutcome: Press says opposition is blocking necessary urban order.

reactionOptions.text: Call for an independent probe into selective targeting of rival-affiliated hawkers.

risk.badOutcome: Probe demand rejected; looks like conspiracy theory.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Kolkata IT Park Attracts First Batch Of Software Companies
newsItems.description: The Salt Lake IT hub signs lease agreements with several mid-sized software firms, creating an estimated eight hundred direct jobs. Boosters claim it can rival Hyderabad and Bangalore if infrastructure improves.

reactionOptions.text: Commit to fast-tracking power, connectivity, and transport for the IT hub.

risk.badOutcome: Power cuts continue; first companies report frustration publicly.

reactionOptions.text: Launch IT skills training for college graduates to fill the new jobs locally.

risk.badOutcome: Training centres lack qualified instructors; scheme criticised.

reactionOptions.text: Demand IT firms abide by labour laws and allow union representation.

risk.badOutcome: Companies threaten to relocate; state is accused of driving investors away.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: River Sand Mining Mafia Blamed For Embankment Erosion And Deaths
newsItems.description: Illegal sand mining on the Damodar and Mayurakshi rivers is linked to bank erosion that has swallowed farmland. A journalist is beaten after reporting on politically-protected syndicates.

reactionOptions.text: Order police crackdown on illegal mining and protect the whistleblower journalist.

risk.badOutcome: Crackdown is selective; allied operators continue; press calls it theatre.

reactionOptions.text: Champion press freedom and demand action against those who attacked the journalist.

risk.badOutcome: Attackers not identified; case stalls; anger fades.

reactionOptions.text: Organise affected farmers to document losses and demand compensation from syndicates.

risk.badOutcome: Syndicate threatens farmers; turnout drops; movement collapses.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Aman Paddy Harvest Breaks State Record; Farmers Ask For Better MSP
newsItems.description: Bumper aman paddy output cheers rural households, but farmers' groups immediately flag that the minimum support price does not reflect rising input costs.

reactionOptions.text: Back the farmers' MSP demand and lobby the centre for a revision.

risk.badOutcome: Centre rejects revision; government looks ineffective.

reactionOptions.text: Announce investment in rural cold storage and procurement infrastructure.

risk.badOutcome: Storage project delayed by contractor issues.

reactionOptions.text: Hold a farmers' felicitation programme and credit state agricultural policy.

risk.badOutcome: MSP grievance overshadows celebration; farmers heckle officials.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Nurses Strike Over Unpaid Salary Arrears; Hospitals Under Strain
newsItems.description: Thousands of nurses in government hospitals walk off their wards after months of salary arrears go unresolved. Patient admissions are disrupted as the state scrambles to respond.

reactionOptions.text: Release salary arrears within forty-eight hours and negotiate a permanent settlement.

risk.badOutcome: Treasury delay; payment bounces; nurses extend the strike.

reactionOptions.text: Stand in solidarity with nurses and demand immediate government action.

risk.badOutcome: Government says strike is illegal; opposition looks like it is endangering patients.

reactionOptions.text: Invoke essential services act to end strike but promise full resolution within a week.

risk.badOutcome: Nurses defy order; confrontation worsens; credibility hits rock-bottom.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Tribal Literacy Rate Jumps In Jungle Mahal Blocks Under Residential School Programme
newsItems.description: Government data shows a significant rise in tribal literacy in Purulia and Bankura following expansion of residential schools. NGOs and teachers are credited with keeping dropout rates low.

reactionOptions.text: Announce scale-up of the residential school model to all tribal blocks.

risk.badOutcome: Rapid expansion stretches teacher quality and hostel conditions.

reactionOptions.text: Felicitate teachers and NGO workers behind the programme.

risk.badOutcome: Ceremony is marred by a strike at a school that day.

reactionOptions.text: Push for Santhali and other tribal languages in the curriculum.

risk.badOutcome: Curriculum reform is opposed by some teachers' bodies as impractical.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State Finance Secretary Warns Of Deepening Fiscal Deficit
newsItems.description: An internal finance department note, leaked to the press, warns that pension obligations and unpaid arrears threaten the state's borrowing capacity. The government disputes the severity but admits challenges.

reactionOptions.text: Accept the warning and announce a fiscal correction roadmap.

risk.badOutcome: Fiscal tightening means cuts to welfare programmes; backlash from party base.

reactionOptions.text: Blame the centre for inadequate grants and demand a special state package.

risk.badOutcome: Centre releases data showing high state expenditure; blame boomerangs.

reactionOptions.text: Argue that tackling corruption in procurement could close the deficit gap.

risk.badOutcome: Government economists say the estimate is wildly exaggerated.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Bengal Village Wins National Communal Harmony Award For Durga Puja Inclusivity
newsItems.description: A Birbhum village where Muslim residents have helped build and fund the Durga Puja for decades receives a national harmony award. The story goes viral and is praised by people across faiths.

reactionOptions.text: Showcase the village as a model and promise to document similar stories from all districts.

risk.badOutcome: Communal incident elsewhere in the week undercuts the feel-good narrative.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an inter-faith village fund to support similar community initiatives.

risk.badOutcome: Politicisation of the fund is feared; communities are sceptical.

reactionOptions.text: Visit the village personally and share the story widely in speeches.

risk.badOutcome: Visit is seen as vote-gathering trip; villagers feel used.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Primary Teacher Appointment Panel Accused Of Taking Bribes For Posts
newsItems.description: Multiple complaint letters reach the education ministry alleging that members of district school selection panels accepted cash payments to manipulate merit lists, placing unqualified candidates ahead of genuine applicants.

reactionOptions.text: Dissolve the accused panels and restart the selection process under independent observers.

risk.badOutcome: Dissolved panel members go to court; appointments stall; thousands of schools remain without teachers.

reactionOptions.text: Organise aggrieved applicants to file a collective petition at the High Court.

risk.badOutcome: Court admits petition but case drags on for years; frustration builds.

reactionOptions.text: Demand written exams and centralised merit scoring replace interviews entirely.

risk.badOutcome: Teachers' union resists written test reform; strike threat emerges.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Teesta Canal Extension Brings Irrigation To Ten Thousand Hectares For First Time
newsItems.description: A long-delayed canal extension is completed, bringing irrigation water to chronically drought-prone areas of North Bengal. Farmers who had relied on erratic rainfall plant a rabi crop for the first time.

reactionOptions.text: Hold a harvest celebration at the canal head and invite farmer leaders.

risk.badOutcome: Canal water flow is disrupted on the day due to upstream blockage; event embarrassed.

reactionOptions.text: Establish water user associations to maintain the canal and ensure equitable distribution.

risk.badOutcome: Associations captured by dominant local faction; equity concerns persist.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a guaranteed annual maintenance fund to prevent canal silting.

risk.badOutcome: Government says fund is already allocated; opposition looks poorly informed.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Brick Kiln Workers Allege Bonded Labour Conditions In Murshidabad
newsItems.description: A human rights group documents systematic debt bondage at brick kilns, with workers from Jharkhand and Odisha unable to leave without repaying advances that compound at usurious rates.

reactionOptions.text: Order police raids on the worst kilns and activate the state bonded labour rescue cell.

risk.badOutcome: Kiln owners are politically connected; raid is called off at district level.

reactionOptions.text: Raise the issue nationally with the labour ministry and demand central intervention.

risk.badOutcome: Centre sends inquiry but no action; national attention fades quickly.

reactionOptions.text: Work with NGOs to set up a rehabilitation fund and legal aid cell for victims.

risk.badOutcome: Fund poorly administered; workers get symbolic amounts.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Pilot Girls' Scholarship Scheme Shows Sharp Drop In Child Marriage In Target Blocks
newsItems.description: An early pilot offering stipends to girls who stay in school past class eight reports a marked reduction in early marriage rates. Block-level data draws national attention.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a state-wide rollout of the scheme from the coming academic year.

risk.badOutcome: Rapid rollout leads to fund diversion; media exposes irregularities.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the scheme be expanded to include stipends for tribal and minority girls.

risk.badOutcome: Government says pilot not yet fully evaluated; expansion seen as premature.

reactionOptions.text: Push the centre to co-fund the scheme under Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan.

risk.badOutcome: Centre declines co-funding; scheme scope is reduced.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Rural Road Contractor Pockets Funds; Freshly Built Roads Wash Away In First Rain
newsItems.description: Roads built under a flagship rural connectivity scheme collapse within months. Soil tests reveal sub-standard materials; the contractor is found to be a front for a politically-connected network.

reactionOptions.text: Commission an independent third-party quality audit of all roads built in the last two years.

risk.badOutcome: Audit finds more failures; political fallout spreads to senior officials.

reactionOptions.text: Publicly blacklist the contractor and initiate criminal proceedings.

risk.badOutcome: Contractor has political allies who pressure the party; case is quietly dropped.

reactionOptions.text: Demand village-level monitoring committees be given oversight of infrastructure contracts.

risk.badOutcome: Government says committees are already in place; opposition looks uninformed.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: West Bengal Achieves Consecutive Zero Polio Months; Health Workers Praised
newsItems.description: After intensive pulse polio drives reaching remote areas, the state records the third consecutive month with zero new polio cases. ASHA workers and district health officials are credited.

reactionOptions.text: Felicitate ASHA workers and call for a revision of their honorarium.

risk.badOutcome: Honorarium revision stalls in finance; workers feel let down.

reactionOptions.text: Use the polio infrastructure to launch a comprehensive child immunisation drive.

risk.badOutcome: Vaccine supply chain breaks down; drive is incomplete.

reactionOptions.text: Credit the community workers and caution against declaring victory prematurely.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as grudging response to genuine good news; unfavourable media.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Land Promotion Scam Surfaces As Kolkata Peri-Urban Buyers Lose Savings
newsItems.description: Hundreds of middle-class buyers in Salt Lake and Rajarhat fringe areas find that plots they paid for do not exist or are encumbered. Promoters with political links are alleged to have fled with deposits.

reactionOptions.text: Demand immediate arrest of absconding promoters and freeze their assets.

risk.badOutcome: Promoters escape jurisdiction; state looks toothless.

reactionOptions.text: Propose legislation requiring escrow accounts for all property down payments.

risk.badOutcome: Builders' lobby opposes law; bill is diluted before passing.

reactionOptions.text: Set up a free legal aid camp for affected buyers to recover deposits.

risk.badOutcome: Legal camp is poorly resourced; lawyers overwhelmed; few cases progressed.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Bengal Athlete Wins Bronze At Asian Athletics Championship; State Celebrates
newsItems.description: A nineteen-year-old sprinter from Midnapore wins a bronze medal at the Asian athletics championship held in Manila, the first Bengal athlete to medal in the event in twenty years.

reactionOptions.text: Felicitate the athlete with a state award and commit to funding their training abroad.

risk.badOutcome: Training funds delayed; athlete joins another state squad.

reactionOptions.text: Use the moment to push for a state sports academy with track-and-field facilities.

risk.badOutcome: Academy land acquisition dispute stalls the project.

reactionOptions.text: Announce district-level coaching camps to identify the next generation of athletes.

risk.badOutcome: Camps lack equipment and qualified coaches; seen as tokenism.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Chronic Power Cuts Anger Industries And Households Ahead Of Winter
newsItems.description: Extended load-shedding in industrial belts and towns triggers protests from factory owners and residents. The state power board blames fuel costs; opposition blames mismanagement and alleged coal procurement scams.

reactionOptions.text: Arrange emergency fuel procurement to restore industrial-belt supply within a week.

risk.badOutcome: Emergency procurement triggers fresh corruption allegations over contract award.

reactionOptions.text: Allege that coal procurement corruption is responsible and demand a CAG probe.

risk.badOutcome: CAG probe not ordered; allegation is dismissed as speculation.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a ten-year renewable energy transition plan to reduce fuel dependency.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as long-term distraction from the immediate crisis.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Tagore Birth Anniversary Celebrations Break Attendance Records Across Districts
newsItems.description: Rabindra Jayanti events this year drew unprecedented crowds in rural blocks, with government-funded cultural troupes performing in schools and maidans. International scholars attend the Kolkata centenary conference.

reactionOptions.text: Establish a Tagore cultural endowment to fund rural arts and literature programmes.

risk.badOutcome: Endowment funds diverted to patronage events; criticism from artists.

reactionOptions.text: Push to include Tagore's social reform writings more prominently in school syllabi.

risk.badOutcome: Syllabus change triggers teachers' union objections.

reactionOptions.text: Commission rural Baul and folk artists for the celebrations and pay them properly.

risk.badOutcome: Urban intellectuals criticise folk inclusion as lowbrow.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Fake Currency Network With Cross-Border Links Busted In Murshidabad
newsItems.description: Police uncover a high-denomination counterfeit note operation linked to networks across the Bangladesh border. Opposition demands to know how long the state knew about it.

reactionOptions.text: Announce enhanced border surveillance and coordinate with central agencies.

risk.badOutcome: Central agencies say state cooperation was late; inter-agency blame war erupts.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a probe into whether political figures facilitated the network.

risk.badOutcome: Allegation of political nexus not proven; opposition faces defamation risk.

reactionOptions.text: Launch community currency-awareness camps in border districts.

risk.badOutcome: Camps underfunded; coverage minimal.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Bengal Wrestlers And Kabaddi Teams Top National Youth Games Medal Tally
newsItems.description: Bengal finishes among the top five states in the national youth games, with wrestlers from Birbhum and kabaddi players from Nadia drawing particular attention from national selectors.

reactionOptions.text: Announce dedicated sports hostels in each division for promising rural athletes.

risk.badOutcome: Hostels promised but construction delayed; athletes feel cheated.

reactionOptions.text: Demand proper salary and benefits for government sports coaches.

risk.badOutcome: Government says pay is under review; no change materialises.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a grant scheme for rural akharas and wrestling training grounds.

risk.badOutcome: Grant money is captured by politically-linked organisers; athletes see little of it.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Embankment Repair Funds Diverted; Villages Fear Another Washout This Monsoon
newsItems.description: A public works audit reveals that embankment repair contracts in North Bengal were awarded without tenders and the money partly unspent, leaving weakened flood barriers before the rainy season.

reactionOptions.text: Order emergency repairs by public works under direct government supervision.

risk.badOutcome: Emergency repairs also poorly done; embankment fails in first monsoon rains.

reactionOptions.text: Initiate prosecution of contract-holding officials and name corrupt contractors.

risk.badOutcome: Prosecution stalls; contractors are found to have party connections.

reactionOptions.text: Demand community-monitored embankment repair with local resident oversight committees.

risk.badOutcome: Government rejects proposal as unconstitutional bypassing of PWD.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Howrah Engineering Clusters Post Record Export Earnings
newsItems.description: Small engineering units in the Howrah belt report record combined exports, driven by demand from Bangladesh and West Asia. Industry associations call for government support on credit access and logistics.

reactionOptions.text: Set up a state MSME credit facilitation centre to help small units access export finance.

risk.badOutcome: Centre is underfunded; only large units benefit.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a state export logistics hub near the Howrah belt to cut transport costs.

risk.badOutcome: Hub land acquisition triggers protest; schedule slips years.

reactionOptions.text: Push for a cluster-based apprenticeship scheme to bring young workers into engineering trades.

risk.badOutcome: Apprentice scheme lacks employer interest; targets not met.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Maoist-Linked Violence Returns To Jangalmahal; Police Post Attacked
newsItems.description: A rural police post in Purulia is attacked, injuring two constables. Security forces link it to a Maoist resurgence feeding on land grievances and unemployment among tribal youth.

reactionOptions.text: Reinforce police presence and launch a coordinated counter-insurgency response.

risk.badOutcome: Heavy-handed crackdown alienates tribal communities; recruits more youth to cause.

reactionOptions.text: Argue that development and land reform are the only lasting answer to extremism.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as soft on violence; hardliners in press condemn the position.

reactionOptions.text: Call for a state-level dialogue with tribal leaders to address root grievances.

risk.badOutcome: Dialogue offer rejected by extremists; peace gesture looks naive.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Pre-Monsoon River Gauge Data Gap Exposes Flood Warning Failures
newsItems.description: A state engineer's report reveals that dozens of river gauges in south Bengal are non-functional, leaving administrators unable to issue timely flood warnings to downstream villages.

reactionOptions.text: Immediately release funds for gauge repair and set a monsoon-eve deadline.

risk.badOutcome: Gauges not repaired in time; flood strikes before warnings can be issued.

reactionOptions.text: Name the officials responsible for gauge maintenance lapse and initiate departmental proceedings.

risk.badOutcome: Officials are union members; proceedings stall under pressure.

reactionOptions.text: Expose the gauge failure in the assembly and demand state-wide infrastructure audit.

risk.badOutcome: Government says repairs were budget-constrained; blame shifts to centre.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State Handloom And Cottage Industry Given National GI Recognition
newsItems.description: Muslin, Baluchari sarees, and Dokra metalwork receive geographical indication tags, opening export possibilities. Weavers and artisans are hopeful but demand better credit access.

reactionOptions.text: Set up a state export-promotion cell to link artisans directly with global buyers.

risk.badOutcome: Export cell is understaffed; artisans still can't access foreign markets.

reactionOptions.text: Guarantee interest-free working capital loans for weavers and Dokra artisans.

risk.badOutcome: Credit scheme captured by middlemen; weavers see limited benefit.

reactionOptions.text: Organise an annual Bengal heritage craft fair to create a domestic luxury market.

risk.badOutcome: Fair is seen as elite event; working weavers cannot afford stall fees.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Free Schoolbag Scheme Distributes Substandard Items At Inflated Prices
newsItems.description: Parents and teachers report that free school bags distributed under a welfare programme are of unusable quality; procurement records suggest materials were bought at twice the market rate from a single supplier with party links.

reactionOptions.text: Cancel the contract, blacklist the supplier, and replace bags before term starts.

risk.badOutcome: Replacement bags also sub-standard; looks like systemic failure.

reactionOptions.text: Raise procurement records in assembly and publish the price comparison data.

risk.badOutcome: Government questions the documents' authenticity; case muddied.

reactionOptions.text: Demand shift to e-procurement with public price disclosure for all welfare items.

risk.badOutcome: E-procurement pilot fails due to internet access gaps in districts.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Sundarbans Biosphere Reserve Expansion Approved; Fishermen Divided
newsItems.description: UNESCO approves a Sundarbans biosphere reserve expansion, drawing international praise. But fishing communities in the expanded zone are anxious about livelihood restrictions and demand compensation.

reactionOptions.text: Celebrate the UNESCO recognition while announcing a fishermen compensation and alternative livelihood package.

risk.badOutcome: Compensation package is slow to disburse; fishermen protest the restrictions.

reactionOptions.text: Propose community-based eco-tourism to give fishermen new income streams.

risk.badOutcome: Eco-tourism benefits accrue to outside operators, not local fishermen.

reactionOptions.text: Side with fishermen and demand the expansion only proceed with full community consent.

risk.badOutcome: Environmentalists attack the position; opposition looks anti-conservation.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: ASHA Health Worker Murdered After Reporting Local Liquor Mafia Ties
newsItems.description: An ASHA worker in Birbhum is found dead after she had reportedly complained to the block health office about threats from a local liquor syndicate connected to political cadres.

reactionOptions.text: Demand immediate arrests and a CBI or CID investigation independent of local police.

risk.badOutcome: Investigation is transferred but stalls; family says no justice has been done.

reactionOptions.text: Champion the cause of frontline women workers and demand a state protection policy.

risk.badOutcome: Government delays action; policy promise rings hollow against backdrop of murder.

reactionOptions.text: Ensure the family receives immediate government support and safe relocation.

risk.badOutcome: Support is seen as hush money by victim's family; media amplifies criticism.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Bengalis Top National Engineering And Medical Entrance Rankings
newsItems.description: Kolkata district students place disproportionately among top national IIT-JEE and AIPMT rankers, fuelling pride but also debate about whether the state's best talent is staying home or leaving for other cities.

reactionOptions.text: Lobby the centre hard for an IIT or AIIMS campus in West Bengal.

risk.badOutcome: Central allocation goes to another state; promise looks hollow.

reactionOptions.text: Offer bonded scholarships for top rankers who agree to work in Bengal for five years.

risk.badOutcome: Scheme seen as limiting talent mobility; educated youth criticise it.

reactionOptions.text: Demand state university upgrade to a global standard so talent stays home.

risk.badOutcome: University unions resist reform; student protests follow.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Biometric Ration Card Pilot Cuts Ghost Beneficiaries By A Third In Test District
newsItems.description: A technology pilot in one district removes thousands of ghost names from the ration list, freeing up grain for genuine beneficiaries. The government announces a state-wide rollout plan.

reactionOptions.text: Commit to a five-year district-by-district biometric rollout with adequate budget.

risk.badOutcome: Rollout is slowed by data accuracy disputes; urban-poor exclusion errors rise.

reactionOptions.text: Set up a robust appeals process so wrongly removed families can re-enrol without harassment.

risk.badOutcome: Appeals office under-resourced; wrongful exclusions persist.

reactionOptions.text: Warn against biometric data being used for political surveillance of opposition-linked families.

risk.badOutcome: Warning dismissed as unfounded; opposition looks anti-technology.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Farmers In Singur Block Road Over Land Acquisition Fears
newsItems.description: Hundreds of farmers in Singur block the state highway, alarmed by rumours that their agricultural land may be acquired for an industrial project. The state government has not yet made any announcement.

reactionOptions.text: Immediately meet the farmers, promise transparent consultation before any acquisition decision.

risk.badOutcome: Meeting ends without resolution; protesters accuse party of stalling.

reactionOptions.text: Defend the state's right to attract industry and call for farmers to await the official policy statement.

risk.badOutcome: Protest expands; opposition uses it against the government in media.

reactionOptions.text: Publicly oppose any forced acquisition without full compensation and consent of landowners.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as blocking development and employment for political gain.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Private Bus Operators Announce Sudden Fare Hike In Kolkata
newsItems.description: Private bus associations raise fares by 20% citing rising fuel costs, angering daily commuters in Kolkata and satellite towns. Workers and students block several routes in protest.

reactionOptions.text: Issue a directive to roll back the hike immediately; threaten permit cancellation.

risk.badOutcome: Operators threaten strike; buses go off road for two days.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a fuel subsidy to operators in exchange for maintaining current fares.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition attacks subsidy as cronyism; fiscal watch groups protest.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the government intervene immediately to protect working-class commuters.

risk.badOutcome: Government reverses hike; party gets no credit for the outcome.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Maoist Ambush Kills Police Personnel In Purulia
newsItems.description: An armed Maoist unit ambushes a police patrol in Purulia's forest belt, killing two constables and injuring four. Senior officers call it the most serious attack in the district in three years.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy additional paramilitary forces and launch a counter-operation in the affected zone.

risk.badOutcome: Civilian casualties reported in operation; rights groups condemn crackdown.

reactionOptions.text: Call for dialogue alongside security operations to address root causes of discontent in the region.

risk.badOutcome: Families of slain officers criticise 'soft' approach; media amplifies backlash.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the government for inadequate intelligence and demand the home ministry be overhauled.

risk.badOutcome: Government calls criticism unhelpful and divisive during a security emergency.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Journalist Exposes Hundreds Of Ghost Beneficiaries In Murshidabad Ration Shops
newsItems.description: An investigative report reveals that ration shop dealers in three Murshidabad blocks have been drawing grain for deceased and migrant beneficiaries for years, pocketing the difference.

reactionOptions.text: Immediately suspend the accused dealers and order a district-wide verification drive.

risk.badOutcome: Verification reveals the problem is state-wide; scale of scandal embarrasses government.

reactionOptions.text: Call for an independent inquiry headed by a retired judge with powers to file FIRs.

risk.badOutcome: Government dismisses call as delaying tactic; media coverage fades.

reactionOptions.text: Defend the ration system as broadly functional and blame isolated rogue dealers.

risk.badOutcome: Second expose names party-linked dealers; scandal deepens significantly.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Dozens Of School Children Hospitalised After Midday Meal Poisoning In Nadia
newsItems.description: Over sixty children fall ill after consuming the midday meal at a government primary school in Nadia. Preliminary reports blame contaminated rice. Parents stage a dharna outside the block education office.

reactionOptions.text: Announce an immediate magisterial inquiry, ensure free treatment, and suspend the school cook and supplier pending probe.

risk.badOutcome: Inquiry drags on; supplier found to have connections to ruling party cadre.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a state-wide audit of all midday meal suppliers and introduce independent food safety checks.

risk.badOutcome: Government conducts its own audit; opposition demand is seen as already addressed.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a state emergency fund to cover treatment costs and compensate affected families.

risk.badOutcome: Fund announced but not disbursed; families report months of delay.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Salt Lake Sector V IT Firms Announce Thousand New Jobs
newsItems.description: Several IT and ITES companies based in Kolkata's Salt Lake Sector V announce a combined hiring drive of over a thousand engineers and support staff, citing lower costs and improving infrastructure compared to other metros.

reactionOptions.text: Claim credit for the IT policy environment and announce an expanded software park.

risk.badOutcome: Companies clarify the decision was purely commercial; government credit looks overblown.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a free skill-training programme to ensure local graduates can access new IT jobs.

risk.badOutcome: Training programme lacks funds; implementation stalls after announcement.

reactionOptions.text: Welcome the jobs but highlight that IT growth must not crowd out manufacturing and traditional workers.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as anti-growth by urban middle class; media frames as backward-looking.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Floods Displace Two Lakh People Across North Bengal Districts
newsItems.description: Unusually heavy monsoon rains cause rivers in Jalpaiguri, Cooch Behar and North Dinajpur to overflow, displacing over two lakh people. Many relief camps lack adequate food and medicine.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy state disaster teams, open relief camps, and personally visit the worst-affected areas.

risk.badOutcome: Relief distribution found to be skewed toward party-affiliated villages; scandal breaks.

reactionOptions.text: Write to the Centre demanding immediate release of National Disaster Relief Funds.

risk.badOutcome: Centre accuses state of mismanaging previous relief funds; blame game delays response.

reactionOptions.text: Mobilise party volunteers to run independent relief camps and document government shortfalls.

risk.badOutcome: Relief effort seen as photo opportunity; communities demand long-term embankment work.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government School Teachers Strike Over Delayed DA Arrears
newsItems.description: Over forty thousand government school teachers begin an indefinite strike demanding payment of Dearness Allowance arrears pending for eighteen months. Examinations in several districts are disrupted.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a phased payment schedule for the DA arrears and call teachers back to work.

risk.badOutcome: Phased payment offer rejected by unions; strike continues another month.

reactionOptions.text: Publicly back the striking teachers and pressure the government to release funds immediately.

risk.badOutcome: Government eventually pays; opposition credit is minimal.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the strike as holding students hostage and demand teachers return immediately.

risk.badOutcome: Teachers and unions publicly denounce the party; electoral consequences follow in teacher-heavy constituencies.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Kolkata Metro Extended To Tollygunge; Commuters Cheer New Stations
newsItems.description: The long-awaited southern extension of the Kolkata Metro opens two new stations, cutting travel time for thousands of daily commuters in south Kolkata. The inauguration draws large crowds.

reactionOptions.text: Celebrate the extension and announce a formal proposal for further Metro expansion to the northern suburbs.

risk.badOutcome: Critics note metro is a Central project; state credit seen as exaggerated.

reactionOptions.text: Welcome the extension and demand government ensure bus and auto feeder services for last-mile connectivity.

risk.badOutcome: Feeder route plan stalls; metro remains inaccessible for many peripheral residents.

reactionOptions.text: Welcome the metro but point to the contrast with neglected rural roads and bus routes in non-urban areas.

risk.badOutcome: Urban voters see the contrast framing as sour grapes; slight media backlash.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Twelve-Hour Power Cuts Cripple Small Industries Across Howrah And Hooghly
newsItems.description: A coal supply shortage at state thermal plants results in rolling power cuts of up to twelve hours daily in Howrah and Hooghly. Small manufacturing units report severe losses; worker layoffs begin.

reactionOptions.text: Declare an electricity emergency, order emergency coal procurement, and provide provisional power to industry on priority.

risk.badOutcome: Emergency procurement at inflated prices invites audit; corruption allegations surface.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a compensation scheme for small industry losses and commit to a new power plant tender.

risk.badOutcome: Compensation scheme under-funded; industrialists call it tokenism.

reactionOptions.text: Publicly accuse the government of long-term mismanagement of the state power sector.

risk.badOutcome: Government points to central grid failures; blame deflected effectively.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Pre-Panchayat Poll Violence Kills Three In Birbhum; Opposition Blames Ruling Cadre
newsItems.description: Three people die in clashes between rival political groups ahead of by-elections to six panchayat seats in Birbhum. The opposition alleges that ruling party cadres intimidated nomination filers; the government denies organised violence.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy additional state police and central observers; announce zero-tolerance for booth-level violence.

risk.badOutcome: Violence continues under police watch; credibility of deployment collapses.

reactionOptions.text: Demand immediate deployment of central paramilitary forces and an election commission-supervised re-poll.

risk.badOutcome: Centre refuses; demand seen as grandstanding without result.

reactionOptions.text: Issue a joint condemnation of violence from all sides and call for a ceasefire.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as wishy-washy; voters punish lack of clear attribution.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State Micro-Credit Scheme Helps Sixty Thousand Women Self-Help Groups Secure Loans
newsItems.description: The state government reports that its expanded micro-credit programme has linked sixty thousand women's self-help groups to formal bank loans this year, enabling cottage industry and dairy ventures across rural districts.

reactionOptions.text: Announce an enhanced second phase targeting one lakh new SHG members in the next two years.

risk.badOutcome: Expansion underfunded; new phase stalls; promises not delivered.

reactionOptions.text: Welcome the numbers but demand independent audits to ensure loans actually reach women and are not captured by middlemen.

risk.badOutcome: Seen as dismissing genuine progress for political points.

reactionOptions.text: Praise the scheme and call for government-backed market linkage centres so SHG products can reach urban buyers.

risk.badOutcome: Market linkage centres announced but plagued by middlemen; rural women see little benefit.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

balanceNotes.publicSupportScale: News reactions use a wider -5..+5 support range. Extreme reactions can produce +5 upside but carry -5 backlash risk; no-comment usually costs -1 to -2 support/media.
balanceNotes.newsToneScale: Constructive/investigative reactions give +4, mobilizing reactions can give +5 with -5 backlash risk, blame/attack reactions can cost -3 support, no-comment costs -2 to -5 depending on severity.
balanceNotes.corruptionScamItems: Items tagged corruption/scam carry higher media and public support stakes. Government silence on such items adds corruptionScore. Swift action subtracts it.
balanceNotes.sportsGoodNews: Sports and good-news items are lighter-weight but offer morale and cultural-connect bonuses. Missing them rarely backfires badly but passing up is a soft cost.
balanceNotes.economyItems: Economy positive items reward proactive framing by government; failing to capitalize is a missed opportunity (-2 support/-2 media). Over-claiming can backfire if delivery lags.
